model_narrative: This is a capacity planning problem for a national petrochemical complex spread across several plant locations over a sequence of multi year time periods. We buy raw crude, run chemical processes on productive units to turn it into intermediate streams, blend those streams into finished refinery products, and make petrochemical final products, then ship everything to the demand regions while optionally importing or exporting some products. Over time we may expand the capacity of the units by investing in discrete capacity steps. We decide how hard to run each process at each plant in each period, how to blend component streams into finished products, how much to ship between plants and out to the regions, how much to import and export, and how much capacity to add to each unit and when. The objective is to make the total discounted cost of the whole plan as small as possible.

sets.name: i
sets.doc: the plant locations where productive units can be operated and where commodities are made

sets.name: j
sets.doc: the demand regions where final products are consumed; the plant locations are also demand regions, so a plant location may appear here as well

sets.name: m
sets.doc: the productive units that can be built and operated, such as the crude distiller and the reformer; each unit hosts one or more processes and carries a capacity

sets.name: p
sets.doc: the chemical processes that can be run; each process consumes some commodities and produces others on a host unit

sets.name: c
sets.doc: every commodity that moves through the complex, including raw crude, intermediate streams produced by the processes, blending components, and the final products that are sold

sets.name: cr
sets.doc: the raw materials that are bought from outside and fed into the complex

sets.name: ci
sets.doc: the intermediate products that are made by one process and consumed by another inside the complex

sets.name: cs
sets.doc: the commodities that are allowed to be shipped between plant locations; only a commodity in this set may move from one plant to another

sets.name: cb
sets.doc: the component streams that are pooled together to make blended final products

sets.name: cfr
sets.doc: the final products that are made at the refineries by blending component streams

sets.name: cfrerf
sets.doc: the refinery final products other than residual fuel oil; these carry ordinary upper quality limits

sets.name: cfrrf
sets.doc: the residual fuel oil refinery product only; its upper sulfur limit follows a time varying cap rather than a fixed product limit

sets.name: cfp
sets.doc: the final products that are made at the petrochemical plants; these are the products that may also be imported and exported

sets.name: cf
sets.doc: all the final products that are sold to meet regional demand

sets.name: q
sets.doc: the quality attributes a blended product can be measured on, such as octane, volatility, and sulfur

sets.name: t
sets.doc: the planning time periods in chronological order; each period spans several years

sets.name: te
sets.doc: the periods in which capacity expansion may take place, which are the planning periods after the first one

sets.name: is
sets.doc: the segments of the piecewise capacity expansion cost function; each segment covers a band of added capacity at its own cost

sets.name: cfcb
sets.doc: the allowed blending pairs; the first entry is a refinery final product and the second is a component stream eligible to be blended into it

sets.name: crcr
sets.doc: the allowed crude oil combinations

params.name: a
params.doc: the input output coefficient of a process keyed by raw source commodity, affected commodity, and process; a negative value means the commodity is consumed per unit of process activity and a positive value means it is produced. The first commodity index tracks which raw stream the activity is charged against

params.name: b
params.doc: the capacity used on a unit per unit of activity of a process that runs on it

params.name: ka
params.doc: the initial installed capacity of a unit at a plant location, in thousand tons per year

params.name: rket1
params.doc: the rate at which existing capacity at a plant location is expanded in the first time period

params.name: kat1
params.doc: the planned capacity of a unit at a plant location in the first time period, derived from the initial capacity grown by the first period expansion rate

params.name: ts
params.doc: a zero or one time accumulation marker that is one when the second period is at or before the first period, used to accumulate expansions and investments made up to and including a period

params.name: op
params.doc: the operating cost charged per unit of activity of a process

params.name: d
params.doc: the share of national demand for a commodity that falls in a region, as a percentage

params.name: tqcf
params.doc: the total national quantity demanded of a final product in the base year

params.name: grfp
params.doc: the yearly growth rate of demand for a final product

params.name: r
params.doc: the demand for a final product in a region in a time period, grown from the base year national demand by the regional share and the yearly growth rate

params.name: tc
params.doc: the transportation cost from a plant location to a region; when the destination is itself a plant location this is the cost of shipping between plants

params.name: pr
params.doc: the import and export reference prices of a commodity, read as the import price and the export price

params.name: pv
params.doc: the purchase price paid to import a commodity, whether a raw material or a final product

params.name: pe
params.doc: the revenue price received for exporting a final product

params.name: eu
params.doc: the upper bound on total exports in a period, in thousand tons per year

params.name: imu
params.doc: the upper bound on total imports in a period, in thousand tons per year

params.name: inv
params.doc: investment data for a unit keyed by attribute, giving a reference size, a reference cost, and a scale exponent

params.name: ss
params.doc: the capacity size added by one full step of investment segment is for a unit

params.name: omega
params.doc: the capital cost charged per unit of investment activity in a segment of a unit at a plant location, already reflecting any small plant cost penalty

params.name: caprf
params.doc: the capital recovery factor that converts a capital outlay into an equivalent yearly charge over the life of a unit

params.name: discf
params.doc: the discount factor applied to the cost of a time period

params.name: year
params.doc: the number of years in one time period

params.name: qll
params.doc: the lower quality limit of a final product on a quality attribute; a value of zero means no lower limit applies for that product and attribute

params.name: quu
params.doc: the upper quality limit of a final product on a quality attribute; a value of zero means no upper limit applies for that product and attribute

params.name: at
params.doc: the quality attribute level that a component stream contributes when blended, keyed by raw source commodity, component stream, and quality attribute

params.name: suurf
params.doc: the time varying upper limit on the sulfur content of residual fuel oil in a period

vars.name: z
vars.doc: the activity level of a process at a plant location in a period, charged against a raw source commodity, in thousand tons per year

vars.name: w
vars.doc: the blending level at a refinery, the amount of a component stream blended into a refinery final product at a plant location in a period, keyed by raw source commodity, component stream, and final product

vars.name: h
vars.doc: the capacity expansion of a unit at a plant location added in a period, in thousand tons per year

vars.name: s
vars.doc: the level of investment activity in a segment of a unit at a plant location in a period

vars.name: xf
vars.doc: the domestic shipment of a final product from a plant location to a region in a period

vars.name: xi
vars.doc: the domestic shipment of an intermediate between two plant locations in a period, keyed by raw source commodity and the shipped intermediate; the third index is the origin plant and the fourth is the destination plant

vars.name: vf
vars.doc: the imports of a final product into a region in a period

vars.name: vr
vars.doc: the imports of a raw material to a plant location in a period

vars.name: e
vars.doc: the exports of a final product from a plant location in a period

vars.name: tcost
vars.doc: the total discounted cost of the whole plan

vars.name: rawmat
vars.doc: the raw material cost in a period

vars.name: operat
vars.doc: the operating cost in a period

vars.name: trans
vars.doc: the transportation cost in a period

vars.name: ccost
vars.doc: the capital cost in a period

vars.name: import_
vars.doc: the import cost in a period

vars.name: export
vars.doc: the export revenue in a period

vars.name: y
vars.doc: one if any investment step is taken in a unit at a plant location in a period, and zero otherwise

description: For each raw material at each plant in each period, what is produced must cover what is drawn down. Add up across all the processes how much of that raw material each process yields or consumes at that plant in that period, charged against its own raw stream, and add to it the amount of that raw material imported to the plant. Require that this combined availability never falls below zero, so the plant never uses more of the raw material than it has on hand.

description: For each blending component stream tied to a raw source, at each plant in each period, the amount made available must be enough to cover the amount blended into finished refinery products. The amount available is what the processes yield or consume of that stream at the plant, plus, only when the stream is one that may be shipped between plants, the net amount received from other plants after subtracting what is sent away, counting only the plant pairs that are actually connected. The amount used is everything blended from that stream into the refinery products that are allowed to draw on it. Require availability to be at least the amount blended away.

model_narrative: This is a capacity planning problem for a national petrochemical complex spread across several plant locations over a sequence of multi year time periods. We buy raw crude, run chemical processes on productive units to turn it into intermediate streams, blend those streams into finished refinery products, and make petrochemical final products, then ship everything to the demand regions while optionally importing or exporting some products. Over time we may expand the capacity of the units by investing in discrete capacity steps. We decide how hard to run each process at each plant in each period, how to blend component streams into finished products, how much to ship between plants and out to the regions, how much to import and export, and how much capacity to add to each unit and when. The objective is to make the total discounted cost of the whole plan as small as possible.

description: Each refinery final product must meet its lower quality limit on every quality attribute for which such a limit is set, at each plant in each period. Take the quality contribution of every component stream blended into the product, weighted by how much of that stream goes in, and require the pooled quality to be at least the product's lower limit applied to the total amount of the product shipped out. Impose this only where a real lower limit exists for that product and attribute, and skip the rest.
expected_pyomo:
def qcfl_rule(model, cfr, q, i, t):
    if value(model.qll[cfr, q]) == 0:
        return Constraint.Skip
    lhs = sum(model.at[cr, cb, q] * model.w[cr, cb, cfr, i, t] for (cr, cb) in model.cfcb)
    rhs = sum(value(model.qll[cfr, q]) * model.xf[cfr, i, j, t] for j in model.j)
    return lhs >= rhs
model.qcfl = Constraint(model.cfr, model.q, model.i, model.t, rule=qcfl_rule)

description: Each refinery final product must stay within its upper quality limit on every quality attribute for which such a limit is set, at each plant in each period. Take the quality contribution of every component stream blended into the product, weighted by how much of that stream goes in, and require the pooled quality to be no more than the allowed cap on the amount shipped out. For an ordinary refinery product the cap is its fixed upper limit, while for residual fuel oil the cap instead follows the sulfur limit that applies in that period. Impose this only where a real upper limit exists for that product and attribute, and skip the rest.

model_narrative: This is a capacity planning problem for a national petrochemical complex spread across several plant locations over a sequence of multi year time periods. We buy raw crude, run chemical processes on productive units to turn it into intermediate streams, blend those streams into finished refinery products, and make petrochemical final products, then ship everything to the demand regions while optionally importing or exporting some products. Over time we may expand the capacity of the units by investing in discrete capacity steps. We decide how hard to run each process at each plant in each period, how to blend component streams into finished products, how much to ship between plants and out to the regions, how much to import and export, and how much capacity to add to each unit and when. The objective is to make the total discounted cost of the whole plan as small as possible.

description: At each unit and plant in each period, the capacity used must not exceed the capacity available. The capacity used adds up, over all the processes on that unit, the capacity each process draws per unit of activity times how much of that activity is run across all the raw sources at the plant. The capacity available is the planned first period capacity of the unit at the plant plus every expansion added to that unit at that plant up to and including the current period. Require usage to stay within availability.

def cc_rule(model, m, i, t):
    lhs = sum(model.b[m, p] * sum(model.z[cr, p, i, t] for cr in model.cr) for p in model.p)
    rhs = value(model.kat1[m, i]) + sum(model.h[m, i, tep] for tep in model.te if value(model.ts[t, tep]) == 1)
    return lhs <= rhs
model.cc = Constraint(model.m, model.i, model.t, rule=cc_rule)

description: For each final product, region, and period, the supply reaching the region must meet its demand. Add up the amount of the product shipped into the region from all the plants, and for a petrochemical final product also add whatever is imported into the region. Require the total to be at least the region's demand for that product in that period.

model_narrative: This is a capacity planning problem for a national petrochemical complex spread across several plant locations over a sequence of multi year time periods. We buy raw crude, run chemical processes on productive units to turn it into intermediate streams, blend those streams into finished refinery products, and make petrochemical final products, then ship everything to the demand regions while optionally importing or exporting some products. Over time we may expand the capacity of the units by investing in discrete capacity steps. We decide how hard to run each process at each plant in each period, how to blend component streams into finished products, how much to ship between plants and out to the regions, how much to import and export, and how much capacity to add to each unit and when. The objective is to make the total discounted cost of the whole plan as small as possible.

description: Define the total discounted cost of the plan. For each period take the raw material cost, the operating cost, the transportation cost, the capital cost, and the import cost, add them up, subtract the export revenue, and discount the result to present value. Sum this across all the periods, scale it by the number of years in a period, and set the total cost equal to that amount.

model_narrative: This is a capacity planning problem for a national petrochemical complex spread across several plant locations over a sequence of multi year time periods. We buy raw crude, run chemical processes on productive units to turn it into intermediate streams, blend those streams into finished refinery products, and make petrochemical final products, then ship everything to the demand regions while optionally importing or exporting some products. Over time we may expand the capacity of the units by investing in discrete capacity steps. We decide how hard to run each process at each plant in each period, how to blend component streams into finished products, how much to ship between plants and out to the regions, how much to import and export, and how much capacity to add to each unit and when. The objective is to make the total discounted cost of the whole plan as small as possible.

description: Define the transportation cost of each period. The first part charges, for every final product, plant, and region, the per unit shipping cost times the amount of that product shipped from the plant to the region. The second part charges, for every shippable intermediate tied to a raw source moving between two plants, the per unit shipping cost between those plants times the amount shipped. Set the period's transportation cost equal to the sum of these two parts.
expected_pyomo:
def atrans_rule(model, t):
    term1 = sum(value(model.tc[i, j]) * model.xf[cf, i, j, t] for cf in model.c for i in model.i for j in model.j)
    term2 = sum(value(model.tc[i, ip]) * model.xi[cr, cs, i, ip, t] for cr in model.cr for cs in model.cs for i in model.i for ip in model.i)
    return model.trans[t] == term1 + term2
model.atrans = Constraint(model.t, rule=atrans_rule)

description: Define the capital cost of each period. Look at every investment step taken in any segment of any unit at any plant up to and including the current period, charge each step its per unit capital cost, add these up, and convert the total capital outlay into an equivalent yearly charge using the capital recovery factor. Set the period's capital cost equal to that charge.

model_narrative: This is a capacity planning problem for a national petrochemical complex spread across several plant locations over a sequence of multi year time periods. We buy raw crude, run chemical processes on productive units to turn it into intermediate streams, blend those streams into finished refinery products, and make petrochemical final products, then ship everything to the demand regions while optionally importing or exporting some products. Over time we may expand the capacity of the units by investing in discrete capacity steps. We decide how hard to run each process at each plant in each period, how to blend component streams into finished products, how much to ship between plants and out to the regions, how much to import and export, and how much capacity to add to each unit and when. The objective is to make the total discounted cost of the whole plan as small as possible.

params.name: imu
params.doc: the upper bound on total imports in a period, in thousand tons per year

params.name: inv
params.doc: investment data for a unit keyed by attribute, giving a reference size, a reference cost, and a scale exponent

params.name: ss
params.doc: the capacity size added by one full step of investment segment is for a unit

params.name: omega
params.doc: the capital cost charged per unit of investment activity in a segment of a unit at a plant location, already reflecting any small plant cost penalty

params.name: caprf
params.doc: the capital recovery factor that converts a capital outlay into an equivalent yearly charge over the life of a unit

params.name: discf
params.doc: the discount factor applied to the cost of a time period

params.name: year
params.doc: the number of years in one time period

params.name: qll
params.doc: the lower quality limit of a final product on a quality attribute; a value of zero means no lower limit applies for that product and attribute

params.name: quu
params.doc: the upper quality limit of a final product on a quality attribute; a value of zero means no upper limit applies for that product and attribute

params.name: at
params.doc: the quality attribute level that a component stream contributes when blended, keyed by raw source commodity, component stream, and quality attribute

params.name: suurf
params.doc: the time varying upper limit on the sulfur content of residual fuel oil in a period

vars.name: z
vars.doc: the activity level of a process at a plant location in a period, charged against a raw source commodity, in thousand tons per year

vars.name: w
vars.doc: the blending level at a refinery, the amount of a component stream blended into a refinery final product at a plant location in a period, keyed by raw source commodity, component stream, and final product

vars.name: h
vars.doc: the capacity expansion of a unit at a plant location added in a period, in thousand tons per year

vars.name: s
vars.doc: the level of investment activity in a segment of a unit at a plant location in a period

vars.name: xf
vars.doc: the domestic shipment of a final product from a plant location to a region in a period

vars.name: xi
vars.doc: the domestic shipment of an intermediate between two plant locations in a period, keyed by raw source commodity and the shipped intermediate; the third index is the origin plant and the fourth is the destination plant

vars.name: vf
vars.doc: the imports of a final product into a region in a period

vars.name: vr
vars.doc: the imports of a raw material to a plant location in a period

vars.name: e
vars.doc: the exports of a final product from a plant location in a period

vars.name: tcost
vars.doc: the total discounted cost of the whole plan

vars.name: rawmat
vars.doc: the raw material cost in a period

vars.name: operat
vars.doc: the operating cost in a period

vars.name: trans
vars.doc: the transportation cost in a period

vars.name: ccost
vars.doc: the capital cost in a period

vars.name: import_
vars.doc: the import cost in a period

vars.name: export
vars.doc: the export revenue in a period

vars.name: y
vars.doc: one if any investment step is taken in a unit at a plant location in a period, and zero otherwise
